name: British Virgin Islands (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: Saint George's Cross and Saint Patrick's Saltire of the Union Flag
the Union Flag fimbriations and the territorial coat of arms

description: A British Blue Ensign defaced with the territorial coat of arms in the fly: a green shield bearing Saint Ursula, robed in white and holding a golden oil lamp, surrounded by eleven further lamps. Below the shield is a scroll inscribed 'VIGILATE'. The Union Flag occupies the canton.
history: Adopted on 15 November 1960 when the territory became a separate Crown colony, replacing the earlier flag of the Leeward Islands. The coat of arms had been granted in 1909.

features.symbolism: the Union Flag, indicating the territory's status as a British Overseas Territory

features.role: coat of arms of the British Virgin Islands
features.symbolism: a green shield charged with Saint Ursula in white robes, holding a golden oil lamp, surrounded by eleven additional lamps representing her eleven thousand virgin companions; below the shield a scroll with the motto 'VIGILATE' ('Be Watchful'). The islands take their name from Saint Ursula and her companions.
